caseResults.validationSummary: The cart page is fully displayed with a product listed, showing name, quantity, unit price, and a subtotal section with currency symbol and numeric value.
improvements: Remove the assertion that checks the cart navigation link text contains '$' — the nav cart link ('Cart1') shows item count badge, not price; price assertions must be scoped to the cart page body, not the nav element

Replace `getByRole('link', { name: /cart/i }).first()` with `page.getByTestId('nav-cart')` for the nav cart link since the element has `data-testid='nav-cart'`, making the locator stable and unambiguous

After navigating to the cart page, assert the total/subtotal using a dedicated locator scoped to the cart content area (e.g., `page.locator('[data-testid="cart-subtotal"]')` or `page.locator('text=Subtotal')`) rather than the nav link

Split the test into clear phases: (1) add item to cart, (2) click nav cart link and verify navigation to cart page, (3) assert item name, quantity, unit price, and subtotal independently with separate expect statements for better failure isolation
Add an explicit `await page.waitForURL(/cart/)` or `await expect(page).toHaveURL(/cart/)` assertion after clicking the cart nav link to confirm page navigation before asserting cart contents
Use `data-testid` attributes for all cart content assertions (e.g., `nav-cart`, cart item name, price, quantity, subtotal) rather than role/text selectors to reduce brittleness from UI text changes

Assert cart item count badge on nav separately using a numeric matcher (e.g., `expect(page.getByTestId('nav-cart')).toContainText('1')`) so badge count validation is explicit and purposeful, not confused with price validation

Add a precondition setup helper (beforeEach or fixture) that programmatically adds an item to the cart via API or direct UI flow before the main assertions, ensuring test isolation and consistent cart state

caseResults.validationSummary: The cart page is fully functional and displays all required elements including product name, quantity, unit price, subtotal with currency symbol. The Cart nav link is visible in the header. Cannot verify data-testid attributes or URL from screenshot alone.
improvements: Add explicit URL assertion after cart navigation: use `await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/cart\//);` to resolve the UNKNOWN check on URL pattern and prevent false positives from in-place drawer rendering
Assert `data-testid='nav-cart'` attribute directly in the test: use `await expect(page.getByTestId('nav-cart')).toBeVisible();` so the data-testid criterion moves from UNKNOWN to a verified PASS/FAIL
Add a `waitForURL` or `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` call after clicking the cart link to ensure full page navigation completes before assertions run, reducing flakiness on slower CI environments

Expand the test to add two or more distinct products before navigating to cart, then assert each product name appears individually, improving coverage of the 'all items displayed' acceptance criterion beyond a single-item happy path

Assert that the subtotal value equals the mathematical sum of (unit price × quantity) for each cart item using a computed locator check, e.g. extracting inner text and comparing parsed floats, to validate accurate total calculation rather than just currency symbol presence

Add a quantity mutation step: increase qty of the existing item via the quantity input or stepper, then assert the subtotal updates accordingly, covering dynamic recalculation which is currently untested

Capture a full-page screenshot on test failure using `page.screenshot({ path: 'screenshots/tc-001-failure.png', fullPage: true })` inside an `afterEach` hook to aid debugging when confidence drops below threshold

Parameterize the test with at least one guest-user and one logged-in-user context using Playwright `test.describe` blocks or projects, since the preconditions list both but the current run only exercises one auth state
Assert cart item count badge or number in the header nav updates correctly after item addition, linking the nav indicator to the cart page state as an additional acceptance criterion coverage point

caseResults.validationSummary: The search input is visible and contains 'John', but no results are displayed — the results container is empty, no speaker results matching 'John' are shown, and no speaker details (name/photo) are visible.
improvements: Fix the assertion on the search input: `toContainText` does not work on `<input>` elements because their value is not text content. Replace `expect(locator).toContainText('John')` with `expect(locator).toHaveValue('John')` to correctly verify the typed value in the search field.

Add an explicit wait for the search results container to appear after submitting the query. Use `await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid="search-results"]', { state: 'visible', timeout: 10000 })` before asserting on result content, since the current 5000ms timeout may be insufficient for async search responses.

Verify the search submission mechanism: after typing 'John', explicitly press Enter with `await page.keyboard.press('Enter')` or click a dedicated search button. Add a `waitForResponse` or `waitForNetworkIdle` call to ensure the search API call completes before asserting on results.

Add a data-testid attribute assertion on the results container (`[data-testid='search-results']` or similar) rather than relying on role-based selectors, to make the results-rendered check explicit and unambiguous.

Assert on speaker result items directly: after the results container is visible, assert `await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="speaker-card"]').first()).toContainText('John')` to validate at least one matching speaker is rendered, covering the core acceptance criterion.

Add a pre-condition check: before running the test, verify via API or seed data that a speaker named 'John Doe' actually exists in the system. A missing data record is a likely root cause for the empty results, and a setup fixture or API call to seed the data should be added in `beforeAll` or `beforeEach`.

Increase the default action timeout for this test to at least 15000ms given the possibility of network latency in the search response, either via `test.setTimeout(30000)` or by passing explicit timeouts to each `waitFor` call.

Capture and log the network response for the search API call using `page.on('response', ...)` to diagnose whether the backend is returning results or an error, improving future debugging when results are unexpectedly empty.

caseResults.validationSummary: The search input contains 'John' but no search results container or speaker cards are visible. The results area appears empty after the search, failing multiple criteria.
improvements: Add an explicit wait for network idle or API response after submitting the search query before asserting results — use `page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')` or intercept the search API call with `page.waitForResponse()` to ensure results have loaded before selector resolution is attempted

Broaden the speaker card selector fallback chain to include common alternatives: `.speaker-card`, `[class*='speaker']`, `[class*='result']`, `li[class*='card']`, `article`, and `[role='listitem']` since `[data-testid='speaker-card']` does not exist in the DOM

Add a results container visibility check before asserting individual cards — wait for `[data-testid='search-results']`, `.results`, `[class*='results']`, or `[role='list']` to be visible with a timeout of at least 10 seconds before resolving child card locators

Capture and log the full DOM of the results area on failure by injecting `page.innerHTML('body')` into the error context, enabling identification of the actual rendered selector structure for speaker result elements

Investigate whether the search requires a full name vs partial match — test with both 'John' and 'John Doe' and assert results independently, as the current input of 'John' may not trigger backend results if exact-match semantics are enforced

Add a pre-test data validation step that confirms at least one speaker named 'John Doe' exists via API or database seeding before the UI test runs, preventing silent failures caused by missing test data

Implement a post-search assertion on URL query parameters (e.g., `expect(page.url()).toContain('query=John')`) to verify the search was actually submitted, distinguishing between a failed submission and a failed results render
Increase the default locator resolution timeout from any current default to a minimum of 15 seconds specifically for the results assertion step to account for slow search API responses

caseResults.validationSummary: The search for 'NOVA Desk lamp' returns a product card showing 'Nova Desk Lamp' at '$74' with a description and action buttons. The search input remains visible with the query. The product image area appears blank/not loaded, and the data-testid attribute cannot be verified from a screenshot alone.
improvements: Replace overly specific data-testid selector '[data-testid='product-card-nova-lamp']' with a broader, more resilient selector strategy: first locate any product card container using generic selectors such as '[data-testid^='product-card']', '.product-card', or '[class*='product']', then scope child queries for the product name within the matched container to avoid hard-coded product-slug assumptions

Add a DOM introspection step before the assertion: after search results load, log or capture all data-testid attribute values present on the page to surface the actual attribute values used by the application, enabling accurate selector generation in future attempts

Use Playwright's getByText or locator with filter to find the product card: replace the failing selector with page.locator(':is([data-testid*="product"], .product-card, [class*="product-item"])').filter({ hasText: /nova desk lamp/i }) to match regardless of exact data-testid naming conventions or casing

Add a waitForSelector with a generic product results container (e.g., '[data-testid*="product"], .search-results, .products-grid') before attempting to resolve individual product card locators, ensuring the results have fully rendered before child element queries are attempted

For the product name text assertion, use a case-insensitive regex match (toContainText(/nova desk lamp/i)) instead of a case-sensitive string match, since the UI renders 'Nova Desk Lamp' while the test asserts 'NOVA Desk Lamp'

Add a dedicated validation step for the product image: assert that an img element within the product card has a non-empty src attribute and does not have broken-image indicators, then mark this as a known visual defect based on the validation result showing the image area blank

Implement a fallback selector chain in resolveLocator that progressively tries: (1) data-testid exact match, (2) data-testid partial/prefix match, (3) semantic role+name selectors like getByRole('heading', { name: /nova desk lamp/i }), (4) text content selectors, before throwing to maximize selector resilience across UI changes

caseResults.validationSummary: The search results page correctly displays 'Arc Mechanical Keyboard' after searching, and the product name is visible. However, the screenshot only shows the search results page before 'Add' is clicked — there is no evidence of a success confirmation, cart icon update, cart page listing the item, or quantity verification. The cart badge still shows 0, indicating 'Add' has not been clicked yet.
improvements: Replace the failing product detail page selector candidates ('.product-detail, .product-page, [data-testid='product-detail']') by first inspecting the actual DOM after clicking a product — use page.url() change detection or wait for a heading containing 'Arc Mechanical Keyboard' via page.waitForSelector('h1:has-text("Arc Mechanical Keyboard")') as the primary signal that the detail page has loaded, eliminating reliance on class-based selectors that do not exist on this app

Add a step to explicitly click the 'Details' button on the product card in search results (visible in screenshot) before waiting for the product detail page — the current script appears to skip this interaction, causing the detail page never to load and all downstream steps to fail

Introduce a post-navigation URL assertion after clicking 'Details' (e.g., expect(page).toHaveURL(/product|detail|arc-mechanical/i)) to confirm the correct page transition occurred before attempting any product-detail-specific selectors

Broaden the 'Add to Cart' button selector to cover the search results page directly — if the app supports adding from the results grid (an 'Add' button is visible on the card per validation notes), add a fallback path: attempt 'Add to Cart' from the card first and only navigate to the detail page if that button is absent

After clicking 'Add to Cart', assert the cart badge count increments to 1 using expect(page.locator('[data-testid="cart-count"], .cart-badge, .cart-icon span')).toHaveText('1') before navigating to the cart, to capture the immediate UI feedback failure point separately from the cart page verification

Add an explicit waitForSelector or waitForResponse after clicking 'Add to Cart' to handle any async cart update API call (e.g., page.waitForResponse(resp => resp.url().includes('/cart') && resp.status() === 200)) ensuring the cart state is persisted before navigating away

Navigate to the cart page after the add action and assert both product name and quantity: expect(page.locator('.cart-item, [data-testid="cart-item"]').filter({ hasText: 'Arc Mechanical Keyboard' })).toBeVisible() and verify quantity input/display equals '1'

Capture a screenshot at each major step boundary (after search results load, after detail page load, after add-to-cart confirmation, after cart page load) using await page.screenshot({ path: `step-${stepName}.png` }) to enable precise visual debugging per step rather than a single failure screenshot

Add the resolved actual selectors (discovered via trace.zip inspection) to a shared selector constants file so the resolveLocator fallback helper is pre-seeded with correct app-specific selectors for this application, reducing runtime resolution failures
